bird.name: 胡錦鳥
bird.summary: 胡錦鳥（学名：Chloebia gouldiae），俗稱七彩文鳥，由於種名是英國鳥類學家約翰·古爾德為了紀念已故的妻子伊莉莎白（Elizabeth Gould）而命名，因此又被稱為古爾德雀或格爾丹雀（Gouldian finch）。胡錦鳥是澳洲的特有種，僅零星分布於澳洲大陸北部…

locations.name: 路德維希·賴興巴赫
locations.summary: 海因里希·戈特利布·路德維希·賴興巴赫（德語：Heinrich Gottlieb Ludwig Reichenbach，1793年1月8日—1879年3月17日）是一名德国植物学家及鸟类学家。 参考文献

locations.name: 约翰·古尔德
locations.summary: 约翰·古尔德 FRS（英語：John Gould，1804年9月14日—1881年2月3日）是19世纪一位英国鸟类学家。1804年生于英格兰，也是一位商人、艺术家。1838年后与其妻一同前往澳大利亚，在当地发现并為许多新物种命名。 早年…

locations.name: 澳大利亚
locations.summary: 澳大利亚联邦（英語：Commonwealth of Australia），通称澳大利亚、澳洲（Australia，缩写为AU、AUS），是一个位于南半球中北部、東半球东部的議會制君主立憲制、聯邦制國家。全国整体属于大洋洲（並非自成一洲），…

locations.name: 金伯利 (西澳)
locations.summary: 金伯利（Kimberley）是澳大利亚西澳大利亚州的九个地区之一，位于西澳州的北部，西临印度洋，北临帝汶海，南为大沙沙漠和塔纳米沙漠，东则与澳大利亚北领地接壤。 该地区以位于南非的金伯利钻石矿区命名，因这两个区域在地形、风景和资源方面均有…

locations.name: 北領地
locations.summary: 北領地（英語：Northern Territory，縮寫為NT），別稱北方领地地区、北部地方、北澳，是澳大利亞的一個聯邦領地，位於澳洲大陸的中北部，北臨帝汶海、阿拉弗拉海與卡奔塔利亞灣，西接西澳州，南鄰南澳州，東界昆士蘭州。其總面積廣達1…

relations.subject: 胡錦鳥
relations.evidence: thumb|right|Black-headed male Gouldian finch Gouldian finches are native to northern Australia, in particular the Kimberley and Northern Territory. Prior to the Australian government's ban on the export of Australian fauna, Gouldian finches were exported worldwide, which has resulted in viable captive breeding populations being held in many countries

relations.subject: 胡錦鳥
relations.evidence: This species has been considered an Endangered species by the Australian Government in the last two "Endangered Species Act"s - the Australian Endangered Species Protection Act (ESPA) of 1992 and the Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation (EPBC) Act, 1999. Its status on the IUCN Red List is currently Least concern, but it was considered to be endangered previously: Threatened in 1988, EN in six assessments between 1994 and 2008, and Near threatened in three assessments between 2012 and 2016. The number of Gouldian finches in the wild decreased dramatically in the 20th century due to human-caused habitat loss.{{Cite journal |last=Tidemann |first=Sonia C
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: 胡錦鳥
relations.evidence: |date=March 1996 |title=Causes of the decline of the Gouldian Finch Erythrura gouldiae |journal=Bird Conservation International |language=en |volume=6 |issue=1 |pages=49–61 |doi=10.1017/S0959270900001301 |issn=|doi-access=free }} The population went from hundreds of thousands in the early 20th century to 2,500 or fewer by the 1980s. The current estimated population continues to be 2,500 or fewer birds. Early research suggested that a parasite called the air sac mite was responsible for the species' decline, but the mite is no longer considered a major factor. The primary threat to wild Gouldian finch populations is an increase in extensive wildfires in the late dry season of its native habitat, which negatively impacts the availability of both tree hollows for breeding, and the seeds that comprise the bulk of the Gouldian finch's diet. Cyclones and climate change have also negatively impacted tree hollow availability in the Northern Territory.{{Cite news |last=Abbatangelo |first=Ben |date=2023-07-11 |title=Binybara is not just home to the Gouldian finch
relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。
